findings.title: Lock files ignored globally — non-deterministic dependency resolution
findings.description: The pre-existing .gitignore contains `*.lock` (line 37), which silently ignores pnpm-lock.yaml, package-lock.json, yarn.lock, and any other lock files. For application projects (as opposed to libraries), lock files must be committed to guarantee reproducible builds and to enable supply-chain integrity tools (Dependabot, Renovate, npm audit) to detect transitive CVEs. Without a committed lock file, every fresh install may resolve different transitive dependency versions, including versions with known vulnerabilities.
findings.impact: Developers installing frontend dependencies get non-deterministic package versions. A transitive dependency could silently upgrade to or regress to a version with a known CVE without any visible change in the repository. CI/CD builds may diverge from local builds. Supply-chain attack surface is increased.
findings.recommendation: Remove `*.lock` from .gitignore and commit lock files. Add targeted ignores for files that actually should be excluded, e.g. `.terraform.lock.hcl` if Terraform is used. For this project: commit `frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml` (pnpm) and keep it tracked. Example replacement entry if any lock files truly should be excluded: `# .terraform.lock.hcl` (not `*.lock`).

findings.title: Missing .env.* variant patterns in .gitignore
findings.description: Only `.env` is listed in the secrets section. Developers commonly create environment-specific files such as `.env.local`, `.env.production`, `.env.staging`, `.env.test` that may contain real credentials. Nuxt in particular uses `.env.local` for local environment overrides by convention (Vite/Nuxt dotenv loading order). None of these variants are covered by the current .gitignore.
findings.impact: A developer creating `.env.local` or `.env.production` with real database URIs, API keys, or secrets would inadvertently commit those credentials to the repository.
findings.recommendation:
Add the following lines to the Secrets & credentials section:
```
.env.*
!.env.example
```
This wildcard covers all `.env.*` variants while explicitly re-including `.env.example` so it stays tracked.

findings.title: Spring Boot profile-specific property files not ignored
findings.description: Spring Boot resolves `application-{profile}.properties` and `application-{profile}.yml` files at runtime. Developers frequently create `application-local.properties` or `application-prod.yml` locally with real connection URIs, passwords, or API keys. These files are not excluded by the current .gitignore patterns.
findings.impact: A developer creating `backend/src/main/resources/application-local.properties` with real MongoDB credentials or a JWT secret would commit those secrets to the repository without any gitignore protection.
findings.recommendation:
Add profile-specific Spring Boot config file patterns to the Secrets section:
```
backend/src/main/resources/application-local*.properties
backend/src/main/resources/application-local*.yml
backend/src/main/resources/application-prod*.properties
backend/src/main/resources/application-prod*.yml
```
Or more broadly: `backend/src/main/resources/application-*.yml` with an explicit `!backend/src/main/resources/application.yml` re-include.

findings.title: credentials.json pattern not covered for non-root locations
findings.description: The pre-existing .gitignore covers `.credentials.json` (with a leading dot, line 9) — a Claude Code internal file. However, the more common Google service account or OAuth credential file name `credentials.json` (no leading dot) is not excluded. If a developer places a GCP service account JSON or any OAuth2 credentials file at any path other than root (e.g. `backend/credentials.json`, `infra/credentials.json`), it would not be protected.
findings.impact: GCP service account keys or OAuth client secrets committed to the repository expose cloud resource access to anyone with repository read access.
findings.recommendation:
Add to the Secrets & credentials section:
```
credentials.json
service-account*.json
*-credentials.json
```
